column4: Description
column5: Purpose
column12: Storage Considerations

column4: Hardcopy Bible with commentary and cross-references.
column5: Anchor mindset, provide moral clarity, and sustain hope.
column12: Keep dry, pack in waterproof bag for Bug Out.

column4: Printed list of church elders, prayer network, and neighbors.
column5: Maintain spiritual community and organize mutual aid.
column12: Keep in physical data vault / Go-Bag.

column4: Pocket-sized guide on Observe, Orient, Decide, Act protocol.
column5: Prevent analysis paralysis and panic during acute stress.
column12: Keep in Go-Bag and nightstand.

column4: Waterproof USB drive containing IDs, deeds, insurance, medical info.
column5: Prove identity, assets, and medical history if home is lost.
column12: Keep encrypted (e.g., BitLocker); store in waterproof case.

column4: Written rally points and out-of-state emergency contacts.
column5: Reunite family when cellular networks fail.
column12: Print and laminate card for every wallet/backpack.

column4: High-calorie, no-cook emergency rations (e.g., SOS bars, MREs).
column5: Sustain energy during immediate evacuation or blackout.
column12: Keep in Go-Bag. Climate-controlled prevents melting.

column4: Canned goods, pasta, rice, peanut butter (everyday foods).
column5: Sustain household during supply chain disruptions.
column12: Cool, dark, dry space. Protect from pests.

column4: Wheat berries, rice, beans sealed with oxygen absorbers.
column5: Prevent starvation during catastrophic infrastructure collapse.
column12: 5-gallon food-grade buckets. Avoid extreme heat.

column4: Stackable, heavy-duty BPA-free water storage containers.
column5: Immediate hydration baseline without relying on municipal pressure.
column12: Store off bare concrete (use wood pallets). Dark, cool area.

column4: High-capacity stainless steel filter (e.g., Berkey, Propur).
column5: Purify scavenged or compromised water for the whole family.
column12: Do NOT let filter elements freeze (they will crack).

column4: Portable filters (e.g., Sawyer Squeeze, LifeStraw).
column5: Procurement and purification while bugging out.
column12: Keep close to body in winter to prevent freezing.

column4: Tourniquet (CAT/SOF-T), hemostatic gauze, pressure dressing, chest seal.
column5: Stop massive hemorrhage before EMS can arrive (or if they can't).
column12: Keep accessible (not buried). Avoid leaving in hot cars if possible.
column13: Check expiration dates on hemostatics bi-annually.
column14: Severe Injury, Civil Unrest, Car Crash

column4: Backup of daily prescriptions, antibiotics, ibuprofen, loperamide.
column5: Treat infections, pain, and illness without doctors/pharmacies.
column12: Store in cool, dry place. Heat degrades efficacy quickly.

column4: Heavy insulating curtains and custom-cut Reflectix panels.
column5: Retain heat in winter, block heat in summer, and mask light from outside.
column12: Roll up when not in use.

column4: Hands-free LED lighting with red-light capability.
column5: Navigate and perform tasks (medical/cooking) safely in the dark.
column12: Remove batteries if storing long-term to prevent acid leaks.

column4: Portable power station with solar panels (e.g., EcoFlow, Jackery).
column5: Recharge comms, run medical devices (CPAP), and power freezers.
column12: Store indoors at room temperature.

column4: Hand-crank/solar/battery radio with SAME technology.
column5: Receive verified government weather alerts and OSINT.
column12: Keep near sleeping area with fresh batteries.

column4: Handheld walkie-talkies for local line-of-sight comms.
column5: Coordinate neighborhood watch and family movements without cell service.
column12: Store with radios off; keep spare AA/AAA batteries.

column4: Waterproof maps of county, state, and evacuation routes.
column5: Navigate when GPS/Google Maps are offline.
column12: Keep in waterproof map sleeve in Go-Bag and vehicle.

column4: Leatherman or Gerber tool with pliers, knife, saw, wire cutters.
column5: Expedient repairs on gear, vehicles, and home.
column12: Oil hinges lightly to prevent rust.

column4: 3-inch or 4-inch screws replacing standard 1/2-inch door screws.
column5: Prevent home invasion via kick-ins (CPTED strategy).
column12: Install directly into wall stud, not just the door frame.

column4: 5-gallon bucket, toilet seat lid, contractor bags, sawdust/peat moss.
column5: Prevent dysentery and cholera when municipal plumbing fails.
column12: Store bags/sawdust in a dry place.
column13: Replace bags and sanitize bucket after use.

column4: Jumper cables, tire plug kit, 12V inflator, flares, folding shovel.
column5: Self-rescue if stranded during an evacuation.
column12: Secure in trunk.
column13: Check inflator and tire plugs bi-annually.

column4: Ergonomic backpack loaded with baseline survival hardware.
column5: Immediate strategic relocation if the home is compromised.
column12: Keep light (under 20% body weight). Store near exit.

column4: Separate bins of cheap bulk rice, water, and baby formula.
column5: Safely provide triage aid to neighbors without starving your own family.
column12: Clearly label to prevent family from eating it by accident.

column4: Non-GMO seeds, manual tiller, composting bins.
column5: Transition from consumer to producer if supply chains permanently fail.
column12: Store seeds in a cool, dark, dry place (freezer/fridge ideal).
